item.title: US Asset Manager PGIM Flips Fed View, Sees Three Hikes This Year
item.description: US asset manager PGIM has staked out the fringe view that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates three times this year to put the brakes on the economy — then reverse the hikes in 2027.

item.title: What Is Anthropic’s Mythos AI and Why Was It Blocked?
item.description: Anthropic PBC has said its new artificial intelligence tool, Mythos, is so good at finding vulnerabilities in software and computer systems that it can’t be released to the general public. The AI giant released Mythos to only 200 partner organizations because if a tool this powerful fell into the wrong hands, Anthropic says, it could help attackers more easily steal data or disrupt critical infrastructure.

item.title: Polymarket Traders Clash Over $345 Million Iran Peace Market
item.description: A proposed deal to end hostilities between the US and Iran gave equities and bond traders a measure of relief on Monday. It also left prediction markets with a new headache.

item.title: BlackRock’s Rieder Sees ‘Explosive’ Rally as Cash Gets Unlocked
item.description: BlackRock Inc.’s Rick Rieder said the surge in US equities following President Donald Trump’s Iran peace deal announcement reflects investors starting to redeploy the roughly $8 trillion to $9 trillion held in money market funds.

item.title: US Spot Petrochemical Prices Retreat as Iran Premium Unwinds
item.description: US spot petrochemical prices continued to soften last week as easing tensions between the US and Iran weighed on export demand, though a series of operational issues along the Gulf Coast helped limit steeper declines.

item.title: SpaceX Said to Allocate $600 Million to European Retail in Blockbuster IPO
item.description: European retail investors bought less than 1% of SpaceX’s record initial public offering, a sliver of the biggest first-time share sale in history.

item.title: Companies Rush to Borrow Cash as US-Iran Deal Spurs Bullish Mood
item.description: US companies looked to raise more than $40 billion of debt in the credit markets on Monday, as they tried to capitalize on investor optimism after the US and Iran reached a tentative agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: Citadel Securities Warns Higher Rates to Challenge Risk Assets
item.description: Risk assets are headed for turbulence as the Federal Reserve edges toward a potential interest-rate hiking cycle and investors grow more discerning about the economic impact of artificial intelligence, according to Citadel Securities.

item.title: US Oil Reserve Hits 43-Year Low as Trump Aims to Tame Prices
item.description: The US’s emergency supply of crude oil has fallen to its lowest level since 1983 as the Trump administration moves toward completing its plan to release 172 million barrels to ease surging fuel prices spurred by the war in Iran.

item.title: Something Big Just Changed in the US Economy
item.description: We’re leveraging up again.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Stress Tests Crypto’s Bid to Reinvent Stocks
item.description: For years, crypto firms have argued that blockchain technology could create markets for private companies long before they reach Wall Street. SpaceX provided the industry’s biggest test yet. The results were mixed.

item.title: JPMorgan Traders Turn Bullish on US Stocks Citing Peace Deal
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s trading desk has turned bullish on stocks following optimism that peace in the Middle East is near.

item.title: Empty Sanctioned Iran Tanker Moves Toward Gulf, Testing Blockade
item.description: A supertanker sanctioned for its role in moving Iranian oil is moving toward the Persian Gulf, testing a US blockade that’s been in place since mid-April.

item.title: Waste Management of Canada Eyes Selling C$750 Million of Bonds
item.description: Waste Management of Canada is looking to sell as much as C$750 million ($536 million) of notes as soon as this week, according to people with knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Anthropic Set to Meet Trump Officials Over AI Security Concerns
item.description: Anthropic PBC executives are planning to meet with Trump administration officials on Monday to discuss an unprecedented US government directive barring foreign access to the company’s most advanced artificial intelligence models, according to a person familiar with the matter.

item.title: Chainguard, Cyber Firms Use AI to Hunt for Open-Source Flaws
item.description: More than two dozen companies including JPMorgan Chase & Co. and an array of cybersecurity firms are collaborating to remedy software flaws spotted by cutting-edge artificial intelligence models.

item.title: Peru’s Economy Expanded Slightly Faster Than Expected in April
item.description: Peru posted slightly better than expected growth in April, driven by strong results in construction, retail sales and manufacturing.

item.title: Banks Vie to Underwrite £5 Billion Intertek Buyout Debt
item.description: Bankers are lining up debt packages of about £5 billion ($6.7 billion) to back a potential takeover of British product-testing firm Intertek Group Plc by private equity firm EQT AB.

item.title: Montreal Transit System REM to Sell Around C$2 Billion Bonds
item.description: Réseau express métropolitain (REM), an automated light metro system in Greater Montreal, is looking to raise around C$2 billion ($1.43 million) in a bond sale as soon as this week, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Industrials Stocks Erase War Selloff, Hit Record on US-Iran Deal
item.description: Shares of large US industrial companies jumped to a record Monday after the US and Iran said an interim peace deal was reached, raising hopes that the energy crisis threatening manufacturers’ and transportation firms’ profits will recede.

item.title: Nuveen Private Bond Chief Scoops Up AI’s ‘Picks and Shovels’
item.description: Data centers and computing chips are all the rage in global credit markets right now, but Nuveen LLC’s Laura Parrott is more excited about the “boring, plain vanilla” infrastructure on the outskirts of AI that will benefit regardless of how the technology pans out.

item.title: IMF Says Still on ‘High Alert’ for War Impact After Iran-US Deal
item.description: The International Monetary Fund said it remains on “high alert” over fallout from the Middle East war on the global economy, warning that energy supplies will take time to recover even as the US and Iran announced an agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: UBS Sees Warsh Catching a Break on Rates With Reprieve From Oil
item.description: Pressure on the Federal Reserve to hike interest rates this year is easing after the US and Iran said they reached a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, reining in oil prices and sparking a Treasury market rally, according to Leslie Falconio of UBS AG.

item.title: Europe’s Alternative to Patriot Missile Is Finding More Buyers
item.description: More nations are evaluating the European-made SAMP/T NG missile defense system, demonstrating the Franco-Italian interceptor’s emergence as an alternative to hard-to-get Patriot batteries from the US.

item.title: US Homebuilder Sentiment Falls, Driven by Large Drop in South
item.description: Confidence among US homebuilders slipped in June, dragged down by rising mortgage rates and materials costs, as well as a sharp drop in sentiment across the South.

item.title: Thousands of Public-School Staff Across US Face Layoff Warnings
item.description: Thousands of US public-school employees have received warnings saying they could lose their jobs as the nation’s education system grapples with mounting financial pressures.

item.title: Adani, Jabil Alliance to Build AI Data Center Gear in India
item.description: Billionaire Gautam Adani’s group has partnered with American engineering company Jabil Inc. to manufacture AI data center equipment in India as it seeks to capitalize on the country’s artificial intelligence buildout.

item.title: Airport Handler Menzies Sees High Jet Fuel Prices Through Summer
item.description: Airport ground-services provider Menzies Aviation Ltd. expects jet fuel costs to stay high for several more months because of fallout from the Iran war.

item.title: Nigeria Inflation Quickens as Middle East War Drives Fuel Costs
item.description: Nigeria’s annual inflation rate climbed to its highest level in six months, as the war in the Middle East drove up fuel prices.

item.title: US Manufacturing Output Stalls for the First Time This Year
item.description: US manufacturing production stalled in May after four months of gains, suggesting problems with supply chains disrupted by the Iran war and soaring costs may be starting to weigh on activity.
